item.title: Deezer reshapes offering to businesses to boost revenue with ads, AI-detection
item.description: Deezer said on Thursday it was reshaping and expanding its partnership ​offerings under a single brand, ‌as the French music streaming platform seeks new revenue streams beyond consumer subscriptions.

item.title: As OpenClaw enthusiasm grips China, schoolkids and retirees alike raise 'lobsters'
item.description: Fan Xinquan, a retired electronics worker in Beijing, has recently started raising a "lobster," hoping that the AI agent he has been training can help organise his specialised industry knowledge better than chatbots like ​DeepSeek.

item.title: Samsung Elec plans over $73 bln investment in 2026 to lead in AI chip sector
item.description: Samsung Electronics said on Thursday that ​it plans to invest more ‌than 110 trillion won ($73.24 billion) this year in research and development ​and facilities in a bid ​to lead the semiconductor industry ⁠in artificial intelligence.

item.title: Prolonged Iran crisis to push buyers to non-Mideast supply, JERA executive says
item.description: A prolonged U.S.-Israeli war with Iran could push buyers to ​non-Middle East suppliers such as the U.S. and Canada, said a senior executive at Japan's biggest power ‌generator JERA, as the conflict spreads to energy infrastructure across the region.

item.title: Morgan Stanley pushes back Fed rate cut forecast to September
item.description: Morgan Stanley ​on Thursday ‌joined major brokerages like ​Goldman ​Sachs in delaying ⁠its ​forecast for ​the U.S. Federal Reserve's next ​interest ​rate cut to ‌September ⁠from June after the central ​bank ​flagged ⁠uncertainty amid the ​Middle ​East ⁠conflict.

item.title: Lamborghini 2025 profit dented by US tariffs and EV U-turn
item.description: Italian sports carmaker Lamborghini on Thursday ‌reported weaker 2025 earnings despite record revenue, after U.S. tariffs, currency moves and charges related to scrapping its announced first fully electric vehicle weighed on the results.

item.title: BOJ Governor Ueda's comments at news conference
item.description: The Bank of Japan kept interest rates steady on Thursday but warned that rising oil costs from the Middle East conflict could fuel underlying inflation, signalling caution over mounting price pressures.

item.title: Eight states sue to block Nexstar's plan to acquire rival Tegna
item.description: A group of eight states filed suit late ​Wednesday in U.S. District Court ‌in California to block Nexstar's proposed $3.54 billion acquisition of Tegna ​that would make the ​combined entity the largest U.S. ⁠broadcast station group.

item.title: Morning Bid: Central bankers sound inflation alarm
item.description: A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Ankur Banerjee

item.title: Investors face cloudier Fed rate view as Iran war grips markets
item.description: Investors are facing a cloudier view of U.S. monetary policy in the coming months, with a war in the Middle East muddying the outlook for a Federal Reserve that already was grappling ​with above-target inflation and an uneven labor market.

item.title: China likely to have standards for post-quantum crytography in 3 years, expert says
item.description: China will likely develop national standards for post-quantum cryptography in the next ‌three years as it pours funds into research, according to a leading expert in the field.

item.title: Apple's China smartphone sales jump 23% to start 2026, bucking industry trend
item.description: Apple posted a 23% surge in China smartphone sales in the first nine weeks of 2026, bucking a broader ​market decline as some Android phone makers raised prices in response ‌to higher costs for memory chips.

item.title: Oil rises 3% after Iran strikes Middle East energy facilities
item.description: Oil prices rose as much as 3% on Thursday after Iran attacked several energy facilities across the Middle East following a strike on ​its South Pars gas field, a major escalation in Tehran's war with the U.S. ‌and Israel.

item.title: US agency asks companies to secure Microsoft tool after Stryker cyberattack
item.description: The U.S. government on Wednesday asked companies to strengthen the security of Microsoft's endpoint management tool, ​after a cyberattack on medical device maker ‌Stryker Corp last week.

item.title: Yen under pressure as focus turns to BOJ after Fed holds
item.description: The yen teetered on the cusp of a two-year low at the start of trading in Asia on Thursday, pressured by a ​firmer dollar as markets watched for how the Bank of Japan will balance inflation risks against slowing growth in the shadow of the Iran conflict.

item.title: Musk says SpaceX AI, Tesla will keep ordering Nvidia chips at scale
item.description: Billionaire ​Elon Musk ‌said ​late ​on Thursday ⁠that ​his companies ​SpaceX AI ​and ​Tesla expect to ‌continue ⁠ordering Nvidia ​chips ​at ⁠scale.

item.title: Stocks slump, oil gains on worsening war in Middle East
item.description: Stocks slid, oil prices jumped while the U.S. dollar was steady on Thursday after major ​escalation in the U.S. and Israel's war with Iran rattled investors while hawkish tone from the Federal Reserve set the stage ‌for the rest of the central bank meetings.

item.title: Janus Henderson clients, staff push to reject Victory Capital's takeover bid, WSJ reports
item.description: Clients and investment staff at Janus Henderson are urging the ​money manager to reject a takeover bid from Victory Capital ‌in favor of a lower-priced deal from Nelson Peltz's Trian and VC firm General Catalyst, the Wall Street Journal reported on Wednesday.

item.title: HSBC mulls job cuts that could impact around 20,000 roles, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: HSBC Holdings Plc is weighing a ​wave of deep ‌job cuts over the coming years that ​could ultimately impact ​around 20,000 roles, or ⁠about 10% of ​its total workforce, Bloomberg ​News reported on Wednesday, citing people familiar with ​the matter.

item.title: Australian watchdog probes major fuel suppliers over anti-competitive conduct
item.description: Australia's competition regulator said on Thursday it has launched ​an investigation into allegations of anti-competitive conduct by ‌major fuel suppliers including Ampol , BP's Australian unit, Mobil Oil Australia and Viva Energy's unit.

item.title: Fed, BoC strike hawkish tones as top central banks convene in war's shadow
item.description: The U.S. ‌Federal Reserve and Bank of Canada both struck hawkish tones on Wednesday in the face of surging energy prices arising from the Iran war, as a heady week of global central bank meetings kicked into full swing.

item.title: Nasdaq receives SEC nod for trading in tokenized securities
item.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission on Wednesday approved a Nasdaq proposal to allow ​certain stocks to be traded and settled ‌in tokenized form, according to a regulatory filing, marking a step toward integrating blockchain-based settlements into mainstream equity ​markets.

item.title: Stryker cyberattack delays surgeries for some patients, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: A cyberattack against medical device ​maker Stryker last ‌week has delayed surgeries for ​some patients, ​Bloomberg News reported ⁠on Wednesday.

item.title: Exclusive: Bridgewater's chief scientist Sekhon to join Google's DeepMind AI unit
item.description: Jasjeet Sekhon, a top Bridgewater Associates executive, is joining Google's artificial intelligence unit DeepMind as its chief strategy officer, DeepMind founder Demis Hassabis said on Wednesday.

item.title: Powell says tariffs keeping inflation elevated, Fed watching energy prices closely
item.description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell on Wednesday described significant challenges in bringing ‌inflation down, from persistent tariff-driven price hikes to Iran war-driven energy price hikes that the U.S. central bank may not be able to "look through" as a transitory shock.

item.title: US oil exports seen rising as WTI discount to Brent hits widest in 11 years
item.description: The discount for U.S. crude futures versus Brent on Wednesday hit ​the widest in 11 years, as attacks on Middle Eastern oil infrastructure drove the global benchmark higher while rising supply ‌in the U.S. set the stage for a jump in oil exports.

item.title: Acquihires, often used by Big Tech, are a 'red flag,' DOJ antitrust head says
item.description: Companies’ efforts to sidestep U.S. antitrust scrutiny through tactics such as “acquihires” - a strategy some Big ​Tech firms use to snap up talent at artificial ‌intelligence startups - are a “red flag,” the top U.S. antitrust enforcer told Reuters on Wednesday.

item.title: Japan, UK drive rise in foreign US Treasury holdings in January, data shows
item.description: Foreign ​holdings of U.S. Treasuries rose in January, data from the Treasury Department showed on ‌Wednesday, recovering from a decline in December as investors returned to the market amid generally elevated yields and shifting expectations for Federal Reserve policy.

item.title: Venezuela, PDVSA bonds jump after US waives certain sanctions
item.description: Venezuelan bond prices rose on Wednesday after the United States issued a general license broadly authorizing U.S. companies to do ​business with state oil company PDVSA.

item.title: Fintech company dLocal sees strong growth, profits in 2026
item.description: Fintech company dLocal ​on Wednesday predicted solid growth for its business in 2026, ‌forecasting that the value of payments it processes will surge 50% to 60% as it scales up with large merchants across its markets.
